app.title: NanoGPT Training Dashboard
lang.label: Language
sidebar.title: Controls
sidebar.get_started: Get started — import your training text files first
sidebar.files_ready: Files ready — go to the Train tab to start
sidebar.model_ready: Model trained — open Chat to talk to your AI
sidebar.hint_import: Next step: open the Import tab
sidebar.reset_hint: Remove all data, checkpoints, logs, and training history
sidebar.reset_warning: This permanently deletes everything and cannot be undone!
sidebar.reset_deletes:
Will delete:
- All `.txt` files in `data/`
- Checkpoints & logs in `out-my_ai/`
- Prepared dataset & temp files
- Training history (`registry.json`)
- Active chat history
sidebar.manage_hint: Selective delete: open the **Tools** tab → Manage section
workflow.status_empty: **Step 1 of 3** — Please import training files (upload or download from URL). Supported: HTML, PDF, EPUB, TXT, ZIP, and more.
workflow.status_has_data: **Step 2 of 3** — Files are ready. Open the **Train** tab and press **Start training** when you want to begin (not automatic).
workflow.status_training: **Training in progress** — Watch live loss and logs on the **Analysis** tab. You can stop training from the sidebar.
workflow.status_trained: **Step 3 of 3** — Training complete! Open **Chat** to talk to your model.
guide.title: How to use
guide.steps:
### Recommended workflow

| Step | Tab | What to do |
|------|-----|------------|
| **1** | **Import** | Upload files or paste URLs. The app converts HTML, PDF, EPUB, DOCX, archives, etc. into plain `.txt` in `data/`. |
| **2** | **Data** *(optional)* | Review imported files, character counts, and delete files you do not need. |
| **3** | **Train** | Choose **Auto (recommended)** — settings adapt to your data size and GPU/CPU. Or pick Fast / Normal / Quality / Custom. |
| **4** | **Analysis** | Watch loss charts and logs while training runs. Auto-refreshes every 2 seconds. |
| **5** | **Chat** | After training finishes, chat with your character-level GPT. Adjust temperature and length in the sidebar. |
| **6** | **Tools** *(optional)* | **Manage** — backup/export model, selective delete. Then diagnostics, test convert, validate. |

> **First time?** Start at **Import** — the dashboard will guide you through each step.
guide.formats_table:
| Format | Example | Notes |
|--------|---------|-------|
| `.txt`, `.md` | Plain text | Fastest |
| `.html`, `.htm` | Web pages, Gutenberg | Strips scripts/styles |
| `.xml` | Wikipedia dump | Streaming parse |
| `.pdf` | PDF books | Uses pymupdf |
| `.epub` | E-books | Reads all chapters |
| `.docx` | Word documents | Reads paragraphs |
| `.zip`, `.7z`, `.tar.gz` | Archives | Nested extraction |
| `.gz`, `.bz2`, `.xz` | Single compression | Decompress then convert |
guide.faq_body:
- **OOM / out of memory** → Lower `batch_size`, `block_size`, or `n_layer`
- **Wrong encoding** → Converter uses charset-normalizer automatically
- **7z not installed** → py7zr is used (no system 7z required)
- **Training slow** → Use GPU if available (auto-detect) or reduce max_iters
- **Loss not decreasing** → Add more data, lower learning_rate, or increase iters
guide.credits_body: This dashboard is a fork of [nanoGPT](https://github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT) by Andrej Karpathy, extended with a Streamlit UI for easy training on your own text data.
import.url_label: Paste URLs (spaces, commas, or new lines — use Prepare to format one per line)
import.url_prepare_hint: Click **Prepare links** to format one URL per line, then confirm.
import.url_empty: Please enter at least one URL
import.flow_hint: Step 1: Add URLs or files → Step 2: Review → Step 3: Press **Confirm**. Training starts only from the **Train** tab.
import.next_step: Import complete. Review your files in **Data**, then open **Train** when you are ready — training does not start automatically.
import.has_files_hint: You already have files in data/. Add more above or go to the **Train** tab when ready.
import.upload_confirm_hint: Review the list above, then press Confirm to convert. Nothing is saved until you confirm.
import.sources_body:
One link per line — copy and paste into the box above:

https://www.gutenberg.org/

https://dumps.wikimedia.org/

https://www.gutenberg.org/files/11317/11317-h/11317-h.htm
data.title: All data files
train.title: Training settings
train.no_files: No .txt files — go to the Import tab first
train.no_checkpoint: No checkpoint found — will start from scratch
train.already_running: Training already in progress — see Analysis tab
train.started: Training started (PID {pid}) — open the Analysis tab for live progress
train.complete_banner: Training finished! Here is a sample from your model:
auto.summary: Auto settings based on **{files} file(s)**, **{chars} characters**, vocab **{vocab}** — device `{device}` (VRAM {vram} MB)
auto.advanced_hint: Values below are set automatically. Switch to **Custom** preset to edit manually.
auto.reason.tiny_model: Very small dataset — using a tiny model to reduce overfitting and memory use
auto.reason.small_vocab: Small vocabulary ({vocab} chars) — smaller model is enough
auto.reason.block_large_data: Large dataset ({chars} chars) — longer context window (block_size 64)
auto.reason.max_iters: Dataset has {chars} chars — estimated {iters} iterations for good coverage
auto.reason.batch_cpu: CPU training — reduced batch size to avoid out-of-memory errors
auto.reason.compile_off_cpu: torch.compile disabled on CPU for stability
auto.reason.low_vram: GPU VRAM ≤ {vram} MB — reduced batch size and model size to prevent OOM
auto.reason.vram_mid: GPU VRAM {vram} MB — balanced batch size
auto.reason.vram_high: GPU VRAM {vram} MB — larger batch size for faster training
auto.reason.lr_large_model: Lower learning rate for larger embedding size — more stable training
auto.reason.lr_small_model: Standard learning rate for small model
auto.reason.dropout_small_data: Higher dropout on small data — helps prevent memorization
auto.reason.preset_manual: Using your selected preset; hardware limits still applied below
auto.warn.cpu_slow: Training on CPU can be very slow for large datasets. Consider fewer iterations or a GPU.
auto.warn.tiny_data: Only {chars} characters — the model may memorize text instead of learning patterns. Add more data if possible.
auto.warn.large_vocab: Large vocabulary ({vocab} unique chars) — training may need more iterations and memory.
analysis.title: Loss & live log
analysis.no_loss: No loss data yet — start training first
analysis.refresh_hint: Click Refresh to update (install streamlit >= 1.33 for auto-refresh)
chat.title: Chat with your model
chat.not_trained: No trained model yet — go to the Train tab first
manage.title: Manage & Backup
manage.intro: Backup, restore, or delete specific parts of your project. For a full wipe use **Delete everything** in the sidebar.
manage.backup_hint: Download before deleting or moving to another machine.
manage.import_model_success: Model imported — you can chat or resume training
manage.delete_hint: Remove only what you need. Type DELETE to confirm.
manage.scope_warning_checkpoint: Removes `out-my_ai/` (checkpoint, loss, logs). Keeps your `.txt` files and history.
manage.scope_warning_data: Removes all `.txt` files and `data/my_ai/`. Keeps checkpoint and history.
manage.scope_warning_history: Removes `registry.json` only. Keeps files and checkpoint.
hint.oom: Out of memory — the model or batch size is too large for your device.
hint.reduce_batch: Try lowering **batch_size** (e.g. 4 or 2) in Train → Custom preset.
hint.reduce_model: Try a smaller model: lower **n_layer**, **n_embd**, or use preset **Auto**.
hint.prepare_data: Data preparation failed — check that `.txt` files exist in `data/` and are not empty.
hint.retrain_or_import: Missing checkpoint or vocabulary — train again or import a model zip from Tools.
hint.use_cpu: GPU not available — switch device to **cpu** in Train settings.
hint.encoding: Text encoding issue — re-import the file; the converter uses charset detection automatically.
hint.more_data: Not enough text — import more files (at least a few thousand characters).
hint.vocab_changed: Vocabulary changed since the checkpoint was saved — start from scratch or delete the old checkpoint.
hint.reduce_eval: Lower **eval_iters** (e.g. 25 on CPU) — evaluation runs many forward passes and can exhaust memory.
hint.disable_compile: Disable **torch.compile** — set compile=False for stability on CPU or older PyTorch.
hint.lower_lr: Lower **learning_rate** if loss becomes NaN or unstable.
hint.generic: Check the full log below. Try preset **Auto** or apply suggested settings.
fail.suggested_banner: Recovery mode — applied fix for: **{category}**. Review settings below, then start training.
fail.suggested_edit_hint: Values pre-filled from failure analysis. Adjust if needed, then start training.
fail.cat.vocab_changed: Diagnosis: Data vocabulary changed vs checkpoint
fail.cat.eval_heavy_cpu: Diagnosis: Evaluation crashed on CPU (too many eval batches)
fail.cat.eval_heavy: Diagnosis: Evaluation phase crashed (memory or batch too large)
fail.cat.no_cuda: Diagnosis: GPU requested but not available
fail.cat.data_index: Diagnosis: Token index out of range in data
fail.rec.killed: Linux OOM killer may have stopped the process — use a smaller model and batch size
fail.rec.arch_mismatch: Checkpoint was trained with different n_layer / n_embd / block_size — architectures must match for resume
fail.rec.use_scratch: Switch to **scratch** mode (or delete old checkpoint) and train again
fail.rec.vocab_mismatch: Checkpoint vocab={ckpt} but current data vocab={data} — you changed import files after training
fail.rec.reprepare: Data was re-merged — old checkpoint may be incompatible
fail.rec.eval_crash: Crashed during evaluation on **{device}** with eval_iters={eval_iters} — this runs {eval_iters}× forward passes
fail.rec.check_data: Ensure `.txt` files exist in `data/` and re-import if needed
fail.rec.generic: Try preset **Auto**, or delete checkpoint and restart from scratch
tools.intro: Tools are ordered by priority: **Manage** first, then diagnostics and testing.
tools.diagnostics_desc:
**What it does:** Runs a quick health check on your environment — Python packages, GPU/CPU availability, data folder, checkpoint files, and common misconfigurations.

**When to use:** Before your first training run, or when something fails and you are not sure why.

**Result:** Each check shows ✅ (pass) or ❌ (fail) with a short explanation.
tools.test_convert_desc:
**What it does:** Converts one uploaded file to plain text **without saving** it to `data/`. Shows the first 2,000 characters so you can verify the output.

**When to use:** Before importing a large PDF, EPUB, or archive — make sure the text looks correct.

**Note:** This does not add files to your training dataset. Use the **Import** tab to save converted text.
tools.validate_desc:
**What it does:** Checks whether the prepared dataset (`data/{name}/`) has valid `train.bin`, `val.bin`, and `meta.pkl` files with consistent sizes.

**When to use:** After `prepare_char.py` runs (automatically when you start training), or if training crashes immediately.

**Result:** JSON summary with file sizes and character counts, or a warning if something is missing.
tools.sample_desc:
**What it does:** Loads your latest checkpoint and generates text from a prompt — same engine as the **Chat** tab, but as a one-shot test.

**When to use:** Quick sanity check after training without opening Chat.

**Parameters:**
- **Prompt** — starting text (e.g. `The `)
- **temperature** — higher = more random (0.1–2.0)
- **max_tokens** — how many characters to generate
tools.registry_desc:
**What it does:** Manages your training history and temporary files.

| Action | Purpose |
|--------|--------|
| **Export registry** | Download `registry.json` — records every import and training run (files used, loss, status). |
| **Import registry** | Restore a previously exported registry (e.g. after reinstalling). |
| **Clean temp files** | Remove leftover merge/prepare temp files in `data/` to free disk space. |

**When to use:** Backup before resetting, or cleanup after many training runs.
error.convert_too_short: Converted text is too short ({chars} characters)
